QF.id: XXVA Sales and Distribution of Research Services_11

question.text: A research entity that has agreed to participate as an underwriter of an issuer's IPO shall not publish research on that issuer until:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI Regulation 18(2) states that a research entity participating as an underwriter in an IPO shall not publish research on that issuer before expiry of 25 days from the date of the offering. **The Trap:** The 40-day period applies when the entity acted as manager/co-manager (not just underwriter) of an IPO. 25 days is specific to underwriter participation. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series XV tests various cooling-off periods. Series IX covers underwriting regulations. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Track these blackout periods carefully — distributing research published in violation of cooling-off periods can create liability for both the research entity and sales personnel who distributed it.
